title: Ignore Rules, Attributes, Line Endings, and Repository Content · Repository Repair Bay
summary: Keep generated output, local configuration, binaries, and secrets out of history while preserving cross-platform text behavior. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a content-boundary and normalization policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Trace ignore sources, directory scope, pattern grammar, negation, tracked-state limits, and check-ignore evidence.
Choose repository ignore, info/exclude, and global excludes from whether a rule belongs to every collaborator or one environment.
Use attributes for text normalization, diff, merge, export, and binary behavior with check-attr evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Repository tracks config.local, build/app.bin, and CRLF script check.sh; .gitignore is added afterward. Decide tracked cleanup, attribute normalization, secret rotation, and changed-platform evidence.

Inspect the Ignore Precedence trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace ignore sources, directory scope, pattern grammar, negation, tracked-state limits, and check-ignore evidence.

content.text: For Ignore Precedence, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Ignore Precedence precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Repository tracks config.local, build/app.bin, and CRLF script check.sh; .gitignore is added afterward. Decide tracked cleanup, attribute normalization, secret rotation, and changed-platform evidence.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Adding a tracked file to .gitignore removes it from history.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace ignore sources, directory scope, pattern grammar, negation, tracked-state limits, and check-ignore evidence. Artifact under review: a content-boundary and normalization policy.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Ignore Precedence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-secret-large-generated-data-155el3" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Secret Large Generated Data into observable performance evidence.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path
